refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: timely updating of the worksite model according to customer requirements. It does not combine multiple functions or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the latency of worksite model updates under supported scenarios and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) meets or is below the customer-defined target (e.g., ≤ 1.0 second).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.

candidate_dps: Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes
Periodic full model refresh at fixed intervals
Event-driven update process initiated by external triggers
Hybrid approach combining incremental updates with periodic full refreshes
selected_dp.selected_dp: Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes

coupling_check.notes: The incremental update mechanism logically isolates update processing to only changed parts of the model, minimizing dependencies and supporting independence from other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Incremental updates can be computed within the 1.0 s latency requirement under all supported scenarios
Change detection is reliable and timely to trigger updates
Complexity of incremental updates does not introduce hidden coupling with other system components
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining the update triggering mechanism and data boundaries to further ensure independence and support modular implementation.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system logic responsible for managing model updates and integration with other system components.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives data inputs that may trigger model updates.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated model tasks to other modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication for update propagation and synchronization.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the incremental model update mechanism is central to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Low containment since Vehicle focuses on physical control; model update logic would diffuse heavily outside.
Partial relevance but model update mechanism is not core; would cause moderate diffusion.
Safety Services is focused on alerts and safety, not model updates; poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The incremental model update mechanism is best contained within the NGHE Solution module, which is responsible for core system logic and model management, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Telemetry and Tasking modules may increase coupling.
If model updates require real-time safety checks, some cross-module coordination with Safety Services may be needed, risking diffusion.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks increase, consider refining the DP to separate the update detection mechanism (in NGHE Solution) from safety validation (in Safety Services) to improve modular clarity without becoming implementation-specific.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes' directly addresses the FR of updating the worksite model within the required timeliness by specifying a mechanism that incrementally updates the model upon detecting changes, which supports meeting the latency acceptance criteria.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Telemetry and Tasking modules may increase coupling.
If model updates require real-time safety checks, cross-module coordination with Safety Services may cause diffusion risk.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The incremental model update mechanism is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, which has a high containment score (8) and low diffusion score (2), indicating strong cohesion and minimal diffusion. Other modules show poor containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the ownership candidate.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by monitoring update latency metrics (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) and ensuring the NGHE Solution module correctly processes inputs from Telemetry and outputs to Tasking and Network Link interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is clear and logical, with well-defined interfaces and ownership, minimizing ambiguity in responsibility for model updates.

recommended_revisions: Monitor interface complexity between NGHE Solution and Telemetry/Tasking modules to avoid increased coupling.

If safety-related validation becomes necessary during model updates, consider decomposing the DP to separate update detection (in NGHE Solution) from safety validation (in Safety Services) to reduce diffusion and improve modular clarity.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and feasibility. The DP 'Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes' adequately addresses the FR with clear ownership in the NGHE Solution module, which shows high containment and low diffusion scores. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Although there are noted diffusion risks related to interface complexity and potential safety validation needs, these are manageable through monitoring and possible future decomposition if safety validation becomes necessary. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition level sufficiently balances complexity and modular clarity without premature further decomposition.

revise.why: Monitor interface complexity between NGHE Solution and Telemetry/Tasking modules to avoid increased coupling; consider future decomposition if safety-related validation during model updates becomes necessary to reduce diffusion and improve modular clarity.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module will maintain high containment and low diffusion scores as system evolves.
Interface complexity can be effectively monitored and managed without immediate decomposition.
open_risks: Potential increased coupling due to interface complexity with Telemetry and Tasking modules.
Cross-module coordination risks if real-time safety checks are required during model updates.
evidence_needed: Latency metrics demonstrating update timeliness (p95 ≤ 1.0 s).
Interface monitoring reports showing coupling levels remain acceptable.
Safety validation requirements and their impact on module boundaries if they arise.
